fragility_points: If the designated settlement source differs materially from Binance and stayed below $2,400, the view is too high.
Cross-venue verification was partial because some fetches were blocked.
key_assumptions: The designated Polymarket settlement source probably tracks major spot venue highs closely.
Source-of-truth mismatch risk is real but not dominant.
main_logical_chain: A major venue already showed ETH above $2,400, so the directional YES case is strong.
But threshold contracts resolve off named sources, and that source was not directly verified here.
Therefore the market should still be high probability, but not quite as extreme as 91.6%.
main_thesis: ETH likely reaches or already reached $2,400 on a major venue, but the market is somewhat overconfident because the contract's exact settlement source was not directly verified.
quote_anchors: Polymarket page: rules define the official data sources used to determine the result
source_quality_view: Good direct venue evidence plus useful contract-surface context, but only medium overall confidence because the governing rules text was not directly extracted.
strongest_disconfirmers: The exact Polymarket rules and named official data source were not recoverable in-tool, so Binance alone is not fully dispositive.
strongest_supports: Binance ETHUSDT 24h high printed 2415.50, above the $2,400 threshold.
The extreme market price implies traders likely already saw threshold-touch evidence.
timing_relevance: This is a short-dated threshold market, so source-of-truth and timing alignment matter more than long-run ETH direction.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact settlement venue / oracle in Polymarket rules.
Whether the designated source already recorded a qualifying print.
what_would_change_view: Direct recovery of the Polymarket rules or the designated settlement source showing a >=$2,400 print would push the estimate into the mid/high 90s; proof the named source stayed below $2,400 would push it lower.
